text: When working with APIs, you need to understand a few key concepts. First, you make HTTP requests, usually GET or POST, to get data or send information. Many APIs return data in JSON format, which is easy to read and work with. Authentication is often required, such as an API key, to prove you have access. Be careful with rate limits, as APIs only allow a certain number of requests over time. It is also important to handle errors when requests fail, like a "404 Not Found" response. By following these practices, interacting with APIs becomes much simpler and more reliable.
